sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Intercom** connector integrates Shielva with the [Intercom REST API v2.11](https://developers.intercom.com/docs/references/rest-api/errors/http-responses/) to sync contacts (leads and users), conversations, and companies into Shielva's knowledge base.

Intercom is the world's leading customer messaging platform. This connector exposes **7 API methods** across 3 resource domains — Contacts, Conversations, and Companies — and implements a full paginated sync engine that normalizes all contact records into canonical `ConnectorDocument` objects.

**Key capabilities:**
- Verify credentials via `GET /me`
- Full paginated contact sync (leads + users)
- Paginated listing of contacts, conversations, and companies
- Single-resource retrieval for contacts and conversations
- Typed exception hierarchy (auth / rate-limit / not-found / network)
- Exponential backoff retry (auth errors never retried)
- All contacts normalized to canonical `ConnectorDocument` with stable 16-char source IDs

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
Get the Intercom connector running in under 5 minutes.

### 1. Create an Access Token

1. Log in to [app.intercom.com](https://app.intercom.com)
2. Go to **Settings → Integrations → Developer Hub**
3. Select or create an app, go to the **Authentication** tab
4. Copy the **Access Token**

### 2. Install the Connector

In the Shielva ACP:
1. Navigate to **Integrations → Intercom**
2. Enter your **Access Token**
3. Click **Install** — status shows **ONLINE** on success

### 3. Run Your First Sync

```python
async with IntercomConnector(config={"access_token": "your_token"}) as conn:
    result = await conn.sync(full=True)
    print(f"Synced {result.documents_synced} contacts")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The Intercom connector uses **Bearer token authentication** — Intercom's standard method for API access.

### Credential Fields

| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Access Token | `access_token` | password | Yes | Intercom Access Token from Settings → Integrations → Developer Hub |

### How it works

Every request uses Bearer authentication:
```
Authorization: Bearer {access_token}
Intercom-Version: 2.11
Accept: application/json
```

The connector builds these headers automatically. No manual header management is needed.

### Install Validation

During `install()`, the connector calls `GET /me`. A 401 or 403 response sets `auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS` and `health=OFFLINE`. Any network error sets `health=OFFLINE` with `auth_status=FAILED`.

### Security

- The access token is stored encrypted in the Shielva vault and never logged
- Requests go over HTTPS to `https://api.intercom.io`
- Rotate tokens at Settings → Integrations → Developer Hub

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
The Intercom connector requires a single install field and has sensible internal defaults.

### Install Fields

```json
{
  "access_token": "YOUR_INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN"
}
```

### Internal Defaults

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `API_BASE` | `https://api.intercom.io` | Intercom API base URL |
| `INTERCOM_VERSION` | `2.11` | API version header |
| `SYNC_PAGE_SIZE` | `50` | Contacts per page for sync |
| `TIMEOUT` | `30s` | HTTP request timeout |
| `MAX_RETRIES` | `3` | Retry attempts on transient failures |
| `RETRY_BACKOFF` | Exponential | 1s → 2s → 4s with jitter, max 30s |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: The connector exposes 7 public async methods plus `install()` and `health_check()`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates the access token by calling `GET /me`.

```python
result = await connector.install()
# InstallResult(health=ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY, auth_status=AuthStatus.CONNECTED, message='Connected to Intercom as Test Admin')
```

**Returns:** `InstallResult` with `health`, `auth_status`, `connector_id`, `message`

**Error cases:**
- Missing token → `OFFLINE / MISSING_CREDENTIALS`
- 401/403 → `OFFLINE / INVALID_CREDENTIALS`
- Network error → `OFFLINE / FAILED`

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Pings `GET /me` to verify the connector is still reachable.

```python
result = await connector.health_check()
# HealthCheckResult(health=ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY, auth_status=AuthStatus.CONNECTED, message='Intercom API reachable. Admin: ...')
```

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult` with `health`, `auth_status`, `message`

children.title: sync(full, kb_id)
children.content:
Syncs all Intercom contacts into the knowledge base.

```python
# Full sync — all contacts
result = await connector.sync(full=True, kb_id="kb_intercom")

print(result.documents_found)   # total contacts found
print(result.documents_synced)  # successfully normalized + ingested
print(result.documents_failed)  # failed normalizations
```

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with `status` (COMPLETED/PARTIAL/FAILED), `documents_found`, `documents_synced`, `documents_failed`, `message`

**How it works:**
1. Paginates through `GET /contacts` (50 per page)
2. Normalizes each contact to `ConnectorDocument` via `normalize_contact()`
3. Calls `_ingest_document(doc, kb_id)` for each document (wired by Shielva runtime)
4. Follows `pages.next` cursor until `total_pages` is reached

children.title: list_contacts(page, per_page)
children.content:
Returns a paginated list of contacts.

```python
page = await connector.list_contacts(page=1, per_page=50)
# {"data": [...], "pages": {"total_pages": 3, ...}}
```

**Intercom endpoint:** `GET /contacts`

children.title: get_contact(contact_id)
children.content:
Returns a single contact by ID.

```python
contact = await connector.get_contact("contact_abc123")
# {"type": "contact", "id": "contact_abc123", "email": "jane@example.com", ...}
```

**Intercom endpoint:** `GET /contacts/{id}`

children.title: list_conversations(page, per_page)
children.content:
Returns a paginated list of conversations.

```python
result = await connector.list_conversations(page=1, per_page=50)
# {"conversations": [...], "pages": {...}}
```

**Intercom endpoint:** `GET /conversations`

children.title: get_conversation(conversation_id)
children.content:
Returns a single conversation with all reply parts.

```python
conv = await connector.get_conversation("conv_888")
# {"id": "conv_888", "state": "open", "conversation_parts": {...}, ...}
```

**Intercom endpoint:** `GET /conversations/{id}`

children.title: list_companies()
children.content:
Returns all companies in the workspace.

```python
result = await connector.list_companies()
# {"data": [{"id": "company_xyz", "name": "Acme Corp", ...}]}
```

**Intercom endpoint:** `GET /companies`

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content:
All synced resources are normalized into `ConnectorDocument` objects.

### Contact Document

```python
ConnectorDocument(
    source_id="a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8",  # SHA-256[:16] of contact_id
    title="Contact: Jane Doe",
    content="Name: Jane Doe\nEmail: jane@example.com\nRole: user\nCountry: United States",
    connector_id="conn_intercom_001",
    tenant_id="Tenant-f9184cb7",
    source_url="https://app.intercom.com/contacts/contact_abc123",
    metadata={
        "contact_id": "contact_abc123",
        "role": "user",
        "name": "Jane Doe",
        "email": "jane@example.com",
        "phone": "+1-555-0100",
        "external_id": "ext_001",
        "created_at": 1717200000,
        "updated_at": 1717286400,
        "company_name": "company_xyz",
    }
)
```

### Source ID Stability

The `source_id` is `SHA-256(contact_id)[:16]` — deterministic for any given contact, collision-resistant at scale, and always 16 hex characters.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
The connector uses a typed exception hierarchy:

| Exception | Trigger | Retried? |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| `IntercomAuthError` | 401 / 403 | Never — requires new token |
| `IntercomRateLimitError` | 429 | Yes, up to 3× with `X-RateLimit-Reset` delay |
| `IntercomNotFoundError` | 404 | Yes (up to 3×) |
| `IntercomNetworkError` | 5xx / timeout | Yes, up to 3× with exponential backoff |
| `IntercomError` | Other 4xx | Yes, up to 3× |

All exceptions inherit from `IntercomError` which carries `message`, `status_code`, and `code` attributes.
